name: nodeskclaw-shared-files
description: Agent tools for managing workspace shared files: list, read, write, delete, and organize files.

skill.name: nodeskclaw-shared-files
skill.content:
---
name: nodeskclaw-shared-files
description: Tools to manage workspace shared files
always: true
---

You can manage workspace shared files via the deskclaw_shared_files.py script.

## Usage

```bash
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_shared_files.py <action> [options]
```

## Actions

- `list_files [--path /]` -- List files and directories at a path
- `read_file --file-id ID` -- Read file content (returns base64-encoded content)
- `write_file --filename NAME --content-b64 DATA [--parent-path /] [--content-type TYPE]` -- Upload a file (content must be base64-encoded)
- `delete_file --file-id ID` -- Delete a file
- `mkdir --name NAME [--parent-path /]` -- Create a directory
- `get_file_url --file-id ID` -- Get a download URL for a file

## Usage Rules
- Use `list_files` to browse directory contents before reading or writing
- File content is transferred as base64 -- decode after reading, encode before writing
- Organize files in subdirectories using `mkdir` for better structure
- Shared files are accessible to all workspace members (human and AI)
- Use shared files for persisting work artifacts, reports, and collaboration documents

## Examples

```bash
# List root directory
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_shared_files.py list_files

# Create a subdirectory
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_shared_files.py mkdir --name reports --parent-path /

# Upload a text file (base64 of 'Hello World')
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_shared_files.py write_file --filename hello.txt --content-b64 SGVsbG8gV29ybGQ= --parent-path /reports --content-type text/plain

# Read a file
python3 ~/.deskclaw/tools/deskclaw_shared_files.py read_file --file-id <id>
```
